hero.subtitle: From St-Maurice (Switzerland) to Santiago de Compostela (Spain) — Roland Crettaz walks to prove that an ADHD brain and overcome addictions are not a life sentence.

projet.title: A physical, neurological and human challenge
projet.subtitle: Three dimensions of the same journey: pushing the body's limits, demonstrating brain plasticity, and sharing a message of hope.
projet.card1_text: 1,600 km on foot, alone, from the Swiss Valais to the Galician coast. Crossing three countries, from the Alps to the Pyrenees, all the way to the Atlantic. An extraordinary human adventure.
projet.card2_text: Neuroplasticity shows that the brain reconfigures itself throughout life. Intensive walking generates BDNF — a protein that promotes neural growth. This pilgrimage is a living neuroscience experiment.
projet.card3_text: ADHD, alcohol, medication, tobacco: Roland Crettaz has been through it all. Today he walks to say that rebuilding is possible — at any age, against all odds.

progress.title: Goal: 36,000 €
progress.subtitle: 1,600 km × €22 per kilometer — Each sponsored km funds logistics, equipment and documentary production.

roland.title: Roland Crettaz
roland.subtitle: Born in Valais, Roland spent decades with undiagnosed ADHD, trying to fill the void through alcohol, medication and cigarettes.
roland.bio1: It was not until the age of 40 that everything clicked: the ADHD diagnosis, the decision to stop substances, the long road to reconstruction. Therapy, reading, encounters — and then the crazy idea of a 1,600 km walk.
roland.bio2: "The Camino de Compostela is not an escape. It is a return to myself. Every step is a neuron reconnecting, every stage an addiction left behind."
roland.bio3: Roland documents every step: YouTube videos, blog, and a book in preparation. His goal: to become a speaker to address businesses and mental health institutions.

docu.title: A Documentary, A Book
docu.subtitle: The journey will be documented in real time. A work that tells the story of reconstruction — not just the walk.
docu.docu_text: Camera on his shoulder, Roland films his daily life as a walker: mountain dawns, conversations with fellow pilgrims, moments of doubt and grace. A film about healing through movement.
docu.book_text: A story of inner as much as outer journey. Blending accessible neuroscience with personal testimony, it speaks to anyone looking to rebuild themselves.

sponsors_preview.title: Join the First Sponsors
sponsors_preview.subtitle: Your logo here, on the 1,600 km journey — in the videos, in the book, in the documentary.

trajet.hero_text: A journey through three countries, crossing the Alps, the Massif Central, the Pyrenees and the Castilian meseta.
trajet.map_subtitle: Click on the markers to discover each stage
trajet.stages_subtitle: From departure in Valais to arrival in Galicia
trajet.cursor_subtitle: Follow his progression in real time
trajet.stage_desc_stmaurice: Millennial abbey, symbolic starting point in Valais
trajet.stage_desc_geneve: Crossing the international city, entering Lake Geneva
trajet.stage_desc_lyon: First major French city, confluence of two rivers
trajet.stage_desc_lepuy: Gateway of the GR65 trail, official start of the Camino de Santiago
trajet.stage_desc_figeac: Medieval town in the Lot, crossing the causse plateaus
trajet.stage_desc_cahors: The Valentré Bridge, symbol of the Quercy way
trajet.stage_desc_moissac: Listed Romanesque abbey, exceptional sculpted tympanum
trajet.stage_desc_saintjean: Last French town, crossing the Pyrenees into Spain
trajet.stage_desc_pampelune: Pamplona, first Spanish city, arrival in Navarre
trajet.stage_desc_burgos: Gothic cathedral, historic heart of Castile
trajet.stage_desc_leon: Exceptional stained glass, last major stage before Santiago
trajet.stage_desc_santiago: The Cathedral del Obradoiro — the end of the road

sponsoring.hero_tagline: Every kilometer you sponsor funds this unique project
sponsoring.hero_subtitle: Your company associated with a project of self-surpassing, neuroscience and human inspiration.
sponsoring.title: Choose Your Commitment
sponsoring.subtitle: 4 levels of involvement, for companies of all sizes. Every euro contributes to funding logistics, the documentary and spreading the message.
sponsoring.bronze_feat1: Logo on the website for 1 year
sponsoring.calc_subtitle: Define the number of kilometers you want to sponsor
sponsoring.contact_subtitle: Fill in this form and Roland will reply within 24 hours
sponsoring.faq_a1: You choose a number of kilometers to sponsor (minimum 1 km). Each kilometer is priced at €10. In return, your company receives the benefits associated with your level (Bronze, Silver, Gold or Platinum).
sponsoring.faq_q2: Can I get a tax receipt for deduction?
sponsoring.faq_a2: Since the benefits are services (logo, mentions), the sponsorship is treated as a deductible advertising expense. A partnership agreement and invoice are provided. Consult your accountant for deductibility details in your country.
sponsoring.faq_q3: What happens if Roland does not finish the route?
sponsoring.faq_a3: In case of premature stop due to a serious medical reason, a partial refund clause is provided based on the percentage of the route completed. All details are specified in the partnership agreement.
sponsoring.faq_q4: How will my logo be displayed?
sponsoring.faq_a4: Your logo appears on the website for the duration of the project and up to 12 months after return. In videos, it appears at the beginning and end. In the documentary, it features in the credits. In the book, it appears in the acknowledgments.
sponsoring.faq_a5: Absolutely! Individuals can sponsor too. The Support page offers packages for individual donations. Contact us directly for personalized sponsorship.
sponsoring.current_sponsors_empty: Be among the first! Your logo here.

journal.hero_subtitle: Reflections on the road, neuroplasticity, ADHD and rebuilding
journal.post1_title: Why walking rewires the brain — what science says
journal.post1_excerpt: BDNF, serotonin, dopamine: during intensive walking, the brain bathes in a neurochemical cocktail that promotes plasticity. This is not poetry — it is biology.
journal.post2_title: Living with undiagnosed ADHD for 30 years
journal.post2_excerpt: Before the diagnosis, there was just that permanent feeling of being out of sync, too slow, too fast, never in the right place. Here is my story.
journal.post3_title: The GR65 Trail: Le Puy-en-Velay to St-Jean-Pied-de-Port
journal.post3_excerpt: The Via Podiensis is the backbone of my project. 750 km of waymarked paths through Auvergne, Quercy, Gascony — names that already sing.
journal.post4_title: Alcohol and ADHD: the neurological link we dare not name
journal.post4_excerpt: Why people with ADHD are statistically more at risk of developing addictions. And why understanding this mechanism is the first step toward freedom.
journal.post5_excerpt: You do not walk 1,600 km on a whim. Here is my progressive training program, my mistakes, my victories, and what my feet have learned.
journal.post6_excerpt: The brain is not set in stone. It transforms, heals, and learns at any age. A simple explanation of a concept that changed my life.
journal.videos_subtitle: Follow the preparation and journey on video
journal.newsletter_subtitle: Receive articles, journey updates and project news
journal.newsletter_note: No spam. Easy unsubscribe at any time.
